title: Masnavi: The Song of the Reed
description: The opening of Rumi's spiritual epic: the reed flute's lament for separation from the reed bed — the soul's longing for God.
introduction: The Masnavi is Rumi's masterwork — six books of spiritual verse that he called 'the roots of the roots of the roots of religion.' Its opening image of the reed flute crying for the reed bed from which it was cut is the most famous metaphor in Sufi literature: the human soul's anguish at separation from the divine source. The poem was composed over the last years of Rumi's life and is considered second only to the Quran in the Persian-speaking world.

translation.original_text: بشنو از نی چون حکایت می‌کند / از جدایی‌ها شکایت می‌کند
translation.text: Listen to the reed, how it tells a story — complaining of separations.

translation.original_text: هر کسی کو دور ماند از اصل خویش / باز جوید روزگار وصل خویش
translation.text: Everyone who has been parted from their source seeks again the time of union.
